hints.edictum: 'edictum' is a noun meaning 'decree' — it is the subject of the sentence
hints.Caesare: 'Caesare' is a proper noun — the name Caesar in the ablative
hints.Augusto: 'Augusto' is a proper adjective/name — it modifies 'Caesare'

hints.to demand: 'to demand' is 'exigere' — 'Exiit' comes from 'exire' (to go out)
hints.to exit (leave life): 'exire' can mean 'to die' but its primary meaning is simply 'to go out/forth'

hints.Present: Present of 'exire' would be 'exit' — 'Exiit' with the doubled -i- marks the perfect
hints.Imperfect: Imperfect would be 'exibat' — 'Exiit' is the 3rd singular perfect
hints.Future: Future would be 'exibit' — 'Exiit' is the 3rd singular perfect

hints.exigo, exigere, exegi, exactum: These are the principal parts of 'exigere' (to demand/drive out)
hints.edo, edere, edidi, editum: These are the principal parts of 'edere' (to publish/put forth)
hints.eo, ire, ii, itum: These are the parts of 'ire' (to go) — 'exire' is a compound: ex + ire

hints.Accusative: While 'edictum' looks the same in accusative, here it is the subject — nominative
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'edictum' would be 'edicto' — 'edictum' is nominative here
hints.Genitive: Genitive would be 'edicti' — 'edictum' is the nominative subject

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'edicti' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'edicti' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'edicti' ends in -i

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'Caesar' — 'Caesare' with the -e ending is ablative
hints.Accusative: Accusative would be 'Caesarem' — 'Caesare' is ablative, governed by 'a'
hints.Dative: Dative would be 'Caesari' — 'Caesare' is ablative after the preposition 'a'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'Caesaris' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'Caesaris' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'Caesaris' ends in -is

translation: A decree went out from Caesar Augustus
